steps.title: Find the interior angle
steps.content:
In the figure, $U$, $V$, and $R$ are collinear. Since angle RVS measures $34^\circ$, the adjacent interior angle at $V$ in triangle $UVS$ is
$$
180^\circ-34^\circ=146^\circ
$$

steps.title: Find the angle at U
steps.content:
Triangle $UVS$ has angles $146^\circ$ at $V$ and $23^\circ$ at $S$, so the angle at $U$ is
$$
180^\circ-146^\circ-23^\circ=11^\circ
$$
This is also angle TUR.

steps.title: Use the isosceles triangle
steps.content: Because $RT=TU$, triangle $TRU$ is isosceles. Its base angles at $R$ and $U$ are equal, so both base angles are $11^\circ$.

steps.title: Calculate the vertex angle
steps.content: The marked angle is the vertex angle of triangle $TRU$: $$ x=180^\circ-11^\circ-11^\circ=158^\circ $$ The answer is 158.
